UF-153-1.subject: Quad-witness 100% LABEL concordance across 4 model providers on multi-axis governance audit
UF-153-1.description: S153 fired with n=4 single-witness synth verdicts (Claude-Opus-4.7 / GPT-5.5 / Grok / Gemini-3.1-pro). All 4 returned LABEL=CONFIRM_CLOSURE. 12-axis Q1 per-axis verdicts agreed unanimously on every axis (with one semantically-equivalent verdict-form variance on M6.CC: 2x CONFIRM_CLOSED + 2x CONFIRM_SUBSTANTIVELY_ABSORBED). 1-step BAND spread (3x MEDIUM-HIGH + 1x MEDIUM). Cross-vendor LABEL concordance at this depth (52 sub-verdicts: 12 axes Q1 + 5 sub-conditions Q2 + 4 sub-questions Q3 + 3 elements Q4 + AMENDMENTS + ABSORPTION_GUIDANCE) is a strong positive forensic signal of substrate quality (sec 1 inlined material was complete and unambiguous) + question-design quality (sec 2 questions admitted clear answers with low interpretive variance) + governance-state stability (the M-axis closure picture is robust enough to survive 4 independent expert evaluations).
UF-153-1.implication_for_methodology: Quad-witness fires (n=4 across 4 distinct model providers) provide stronger evidence than dual-witness for high-stakes governance decisions. The dual-witness M-axis cascade rule (cascade 123 sec 3.2) extends naturally to n=4: most-conservative LABEL + most-conservative BAND + most-conservative sub-verdicts. Useful pattern for future RULE-1-lift-class audits or final-paper-distribution sign-off.
UF-153-1.operator_action: none required; positive signal; surface for project memory consideration

UF-153-2.subject: First n=4 quad-witness fire in bridge history; aggregation rule extension precedent
UF-153-2.description: Prior precedent ladder: cascade 123 (n=1 single-witness M7 V0) -> cascade 127R (n=2 dual-witness M8a V0) -> cascade 130R (n=3 dual-witness M8b V0; LABEL-rule extension noted in dual-witness M-axis cascade memory) -> THIS FIRE = cascade 153 (n=4 quad-witness M1-M12 audit). The aggregation rule's most-conservative-everything semantics extend cleanly to n=4 with no methodological pathology. This formally establishes the bridge precedent for quad-witness fires; future fires can cite n=4 as a viable witness mode for governance-evidentiary-class audits where cross-vendor variance is a concern.
UF-153-2.implication_for_methodology: n=4 is a useful 'governance ceiling' witness count. Beyond n=4 marginal signal-vs-cost ratio decreases (the 4 witnesses already span 4 distinct foundational labs: Anthropic + OpenAI + xAI + Google; adding more witnesses adds same-vendor variance instead of cross-vendor coverage). For RULE-1-class lift gates, the project should consider n=4 the 'maximum useful witness count' and use n=2 or n=3 for axis-level V0 cascades.
UF-153-2.operator_action: none required; positive signal; surface for project memory consideration (memory candidate: 'For governance-evidentiary fires (RULE-1-lift class), n=4 quad-witness is a viable ceiling; n=2 dual-witness sufficient for axis-level V0 cascades.')

UF-153-3.subject: Cross-witness convergence on Q4(4a) highest-risk axis = M8b (3/4 witnesses)
UF-153-3.description: Witnesses 1 (Claude), 2 (GPT), and 4 (Gemini) independently identified M8b as the highest-risk axis for post-lift math-regression risk (d>=3-CAVEAT-CARRIED-FORWARD as the most likely scrutiny target). Witness 3 (Grok) chose M10 (tooling-state axis with explicit open work-stream) but acknowledged M8b as a co-risk. Cross-witness convergence on M8b indicates the d>=3 caveat is the most exposed structural boundary in the closure substrate, despite being formally non-blocking. This is consistent with the slot 130R closure annotation explicitly carrying d>=3 forward as a known-residual.
UF-153-3.implication_for_methodology: Operator should treat the d>=3 caveat as a first-priority post-lift disclosure item: explicit caveat tagging in Zenodo deposit metadata + venue cover letter + reproduction checklist (per Q4(4c) aggregated mitigation). M10 sorry-discharge progression is a secondary post-lift monitoring item; the 2026-08-02 status-report cadence already handles this.
UF-153-3.operator_action: incorporate Q4(4c) aggregated mitigation into post-RULE-1-lift admin runbook (D-153-2 + D-153-3 cross-link)
